input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #6518 [Automation] javac compile fails for com.squareup.okhttp3:okhttp:3.8.1; label=fails-javac-compile; body_chars=8015

risks: Existing high-coverage tests for OkHttp platform internals use local stub classes for optional Android and Jetty ALPN reflective branches; those stubs are useful test code if preserving that coverage, but they are not an external dependency, Docker image, or environment/system property setup.

Native-image verification may still require preserving existing native build configuration such as all-charsets support for `okhttp3.internal.Util`, but this is already part of the existing scaffold rather than new deterministic setup.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: OkHttp 3.8.1 has only normal transitive library needs: `okio` is declared by the artifact, while Android and JSR-305 are provided/optional compile-time references. Normal meaningful tests can exercise `OkHttpClient`, requests, responses, URLs, TLS/platform selection, and internal utility behavior without a Docker service, extra Maven test dependency, or environment/system property. The reported failure is an API drift in the generated/copied test (`Util.intersect` now takes a `Comparator`, not `Class<T>`), not missing setup.
